entries.description: Read all five pre-audit input artifacts in docs/proposals/2026-05-19-orchestrator-tracking-architecture/: proposal.md (v1 design), proposal-addendum.md (v1.5 with operator clarifications + responses to GPT R1), consensus-gemini-pro.txt (R1 verdict — broad migration support), consensus-gpt-5-4.txt (R1 verdict — not approved; 3 Highs + 2 OQs), consensus-gpt-5-4-round-2.txt (R2 verdict — credible direction; 3 new must-fix Highs + 2 open questions). Verified the README's must-resolve list matches the items spec.md calls out (H1/H2/H3/OQ1/OQ2).

Authored docs/proposals/2026-05-19-orchestrator-tracking-architecture/audit-resolution-request.md (9.5k chars; spec's 2-4k estimate proved low). Each of H1/H2/H3/OQ1/OQ2 carries pre-audit recommended verdict + rationale + implication + reject-alternative call-out. Section 4 explicitly fences out-of-scope items (direction, queueing/polling, Lightweight tier check-in). Authored route_audit_resolution.py per the existing route_consensus.py pattern (with feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling JSON-dump-first invariant). Ran: Gemini Pro succeeded ($0.008, 2572 in / 520 out); GPT-5.4 hit 429 (3 attempts; same pre-audit pattern). Per spec's manual-paste fallback, generated audit-resolution-paste-for-gpt-5-4.md (__OTHER__ → 'Gemini Pro' substituted) and handed off to operator.

Read both engines' responses. Gemini Pro confirmed all 5 originals (H1, H2, H3, OQ1, OQ2). GPT-5.4 confirmed all 5 originals AND raised a sixth must-resolve item H4 (holder identity key — given the orchestrator block's mutable fields, conflict-equality needs explicit rules). Authored audit-resolution-h4-request.md (4.3k chars) + route_h4_gemini.py to get Gemini Pro's H4 verdict. Gemini REFINED my engine-only proposal to engine + provider composite (future-proofing for Claude-via-Anthropic vs. Claude-via-Bedrock; zero implementation cost since provider already present). Escalated divergence to operator via AskUserQuestion: GPT permitted any stable subset, Gemini specified composite, operator adjudicated = lock composite.

Updated proposal-addendum.md with new §9 'Audit resolution (Set 032 Session 1, 2026-05-19)' capturing all 6 locked verdicts with cross-provider trail. Updated README.md: status header flipped to 'audit resolved'; 'Must-resolve in the audit-then-spec cycle' section replaced with 'Audit resolution — Set 032 Session 1' section; H1/H2/H3/H4/OQ1/OQ2 listed with RESOLVED tags + cross-provider state; cost record extended with Session 1 spend; What ships across Sets 032 + 033 rephrased past-tense for the audit work. Round-2 questions block preserved as HISTORICAL block (superseded by §9). Audit artifacts table extended with the 7 new files.

Authored verify_session1.py (gemini-pro-forced via ai_router.query rather than ai_router.route, since route() picked gpt-5-4 from this bundle's complexity score and gpt-5-4 was 429-blocked). Bundle: audit-resolution-request.md + audit-resolution-h4-request.md + addendum §9 + README resolved section + all three engine response files (28.4k chars). Round A REJECTED with one must-fix: H3 refusal-error-content (the audit packet specified the refusal error must name holder + release paths, but the addendum §9 / README only listed the release paths without saying the error message must contain them). Patched both files: 'The refusal must surface a clear error message naming (a) the current holder (engine + provider) and (b) the two release paths (--force, Release Check-Out)'.

H3 refusal-error-content fix applied to addendum §9 AND README. Re-verified via Round B (gemini-pro $0.019 / 8098 in / 863 out) — all 6 verdicts VERIFIED with quoted cross-reference to both source files for each. No new must-fix items surfaced.

Authored docs/session-sets/033-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-implementation/spec.md — REPLACES the placeholder. 6-session implementation plan translating the 6 locked verdicts (H1-H4, OQ1, OQ2) from Set 032 Session 1 into concrete per-session Steps/Creates/Touches/Ends-with/Progress-keys. Per-session split per Session 1's ai-assignment.md recommendation: S1 state machine + start_session refactor, S2 marker retirement + resolver refactor + banner removal, S3 UI rename + Command Palette release action + Claude hook refactor to invoker, S4 Layer-3 Playwright coverage, S5 queueing/polling, S6 cross-tier close-out check-in + canonical doc updates + cross-repo notification + PyPI + Marketplace release. Updated docs/session-sets/033-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-implementation/session-state.json to align titles with the authored spec (totalSessions: 6; titles replace '(placeholder)' suffixes).

Authored docs/session-sets/032-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-audit/spec-review-request.md (narrow 5-axis prompt: verdict traceability, sequencing, missing surface area, scope creep, risk coverage) + route_spec_review.py (gemini-pro forced via ai_router.query per Session 1 routing pattern). Routed the drafted Set 033 spec.md through Gemini Pro ($0.020, 10643 in / 677 out). Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-SUGGESTIONS. Two non-blocking suggestions: (1) S6 Step 1 close_session idempotence on already-null orchestrator block; (2) Risks section R7 listInProgressSets() performance in repos with many session sets + benchmark note. GPT-5.4 manual-paste not invoked — Gemini's verdict was approve-not-must-fix and suggestions were small.

Applied both cross-review suggestions to docs/session-sets/033-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-implementation/spec.md: (1) S6 Step 1 extended with explicit Idempotence bullet — close_session on a set whose orchestrator block is already null is a successful no-op for the check-in step (close-out's other writes proceed normally); (2) Risks section extended with R7 — listInProgressSets() performance in large repos, with mitigation note (one-time benchmark at Set 033 start + cached index keyed on session-state.json mtime if synthetic 200-set fixture exceeds ~50 ms per scan).

Updated docs/proposals/2026-05-19-orchestrator-tracking-architecture/README.md: status header flipped from 'audit resolved' to 'audit-then-spec cycle complete' with explicit pointer to the authored Set 033 spec.md (replacing the 'placeholder spec currently' note). Artifact table extended with Set 032 Session 2 cross-review artifacts (spec-review-request.md, spec-review-gemini-pro.{txt,json}, route_spec_review.py). Cost record extended with Session 1 verification ($0.032) + Session 2 spec cross-review ($0.020); total architectural-decision spend ~$0.079. 'What ships across Sets 032 + 033' reframed past-tense for the audit + spec-authoring work.

Authored verify_session2.py (gemini-pro-forced via ai_router.query, same pattern as verify_session1.py). Bundle (62.6k chars): Set 033 spec.md + Set 033 session-state.json + audit-input README.md + Set 032 change-log.md + proposal-addendum §9 (ground truth — 6 locked verdicts) + spec-review-gemini-pro.txt (ground truth — cross-review verdict that we applied). Round A VERIFIED with no must-fix items (gemini-pro $0.038, 20440 in / 1269 out). All 6 verdicts traced with quoted citations from the spec; both cross-review suggestions confirmed applied; per-session sequencing internally consistent (S1 writer -> S2 reader -> S3 UI -> S4 tests -> S5 queueing -> S6 close-out); supporting artifacts honest. No load-bearing omissions. Round B not required.

Authored docs/session-sets/032-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-audit/change-log.md — final-session aggregation pattern per [[project_final_session_changelog_pre_close]]. Captures Session 1's audit-resolution work (H1-H4, OQ1, OQ2 locked via cross-engine consensus + operator H4 adjudication) and Session 2's spec-authoring + cross-review + suggestion-application work. Cost section reflects actual ~$0.102 routed spend (S1 $0.044 incl. verification rounds + S2 $0.058 = $0.020 spec cross-review + $0.038 verification). Set 032 closes inside its $0.10-$0.40 forecast band; ~$0.90 of the $1.00 NTE headroom remains. Pattern notes preserved: manual-paste GPT fallback is now durable; ai_router.query over .route() for deterministic Gemini calls; audit-then-spec cycle worked as designed (caught H4 mid-audit which a single combined set would have shipped without).
